title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Stakeholder Container Mission
summary: A municipal service-status team. The team needs a small accessible status application packaged with an immutable base, minimal context, predictable process contract, non-root runtime, and changed-argument tests. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Status Application Image with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Tag Digest Reference
steps.instruction:
project case — A project build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts.

What must be established before implementation?

The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract into observable performance evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract-43yeb9
  - competency: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: dockerfile cmd entrypoint contract
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: cancel during export after one platform finishes
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: 43yeb9
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
